description: Benchmark Default Time Controls - Standardize LTC defaults for match benchmarks, STC defaults for SPRT and fast benchmark passes, and persist time control metadata in benchmark artifacts.

userStories.title: Set STC/LTC defaults in chess-runner
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `chess-runner match` and `chess-runner sprt` to start with practical internal benchmark defaults so that I can run evaluations without retyping the same time controls.
acceptanceCriteria: `chess-runner match` defaults to `--time 60000` and `--inc 1000`
`chess-runner sprt` defaults to `--time 10000` and `--inc 100`
Explicit `--time` and `--inc` CLI overrides continue to work for both subcommands
`chess-runner match --help` and `chess-runner sprt --help` show the new default values
`cargo build --workspace` passes
`cargo test --workspace -- --skip gen_files::magics::name` passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add time-control column to match CSV writer
userStories.description: As a developer, I want match CSV outputs to store time control in a dedicated column so that benchmark-produced LTC and STC rows remain distinguishable without label parsing.
acceptanceCriteria: `write_csv` adds a dedicated `time_control` column to match CSV output
Call sites that write match CSV rows pass explicit `LTC` or `STC` values when appropriate
Existing `start_fen` recording continues to work alongside the new `time_control` column
`cargo build --workspace` passes
`cargo test --workspace -- --skip gen_files::magics::name` passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add time-control column to SPRT CSV writer
userStories.description: As a developer, I want SPRT CSV outputs to store time control in a dedicated column so that benchmark STC comparison runs are explicit in persisted artifacts.
acceptanceCriteria: `write_sprt_csv` adds a dedicated `time_control` column to SPRT CSV output
Call sites that write benchmark SPRT rows pass explicit `STC` values
`cargo build --workspace` passes
`cargo test --workspace -- --skip gen_files::magics::name` passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Run main benchmark match block in LTC
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `benchmark-version.sh` to run its primary match benchmark block in LTC so that the default benchmark reflects the project's slower baseline evaluation mode.
acceptanceCriteria: `scripts/benchmark-version.sh` runs the primary Stockfish match block at `60000ms + 1000ms` by default
The primary block still runs all four Stockfish levels: 1500, 2000, 2500, and max
The script prints that the primary benchmark block is using `LTC` before games start
Rows written by the primary benchmark block record `LTC` in the dedicated match CSV `time_control` column
`bash -n scripts/benchmark-version.sh` passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Run additional benchmark match block in STC
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `benchmark-version.sh` to run an extra STC match block after the LTC block so that each benchmark run captures both slower baseline and faster validation results.
acceptanceCriteria: After the LTC match block, `scripts/benchmark-version.sh` runs a second Stockfish match block at `10000ms + 100ms` by default
The additional STC block still runs all four Stockfish levels: 1500, 2000, 2500, and max
The script prints that the additional benchmark block is using `STC` before games start
Rows written by the additional block record `STC` in the dedicated match CSV `time_control` column
Balanced-position benchmark rows remain distinguishable and also carry the correct `time_control` value
`bash -n scripts/benchmark-version.sh` passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Run benchmark SPRT in STC
userStories.description: As a developer, I want benchmark SPRT runs to use STC by default so that regression checks finish quickly enough for internal iteration.
acceptanceCriteria: When `--prev-engine` is provided, `scripts/benchmark-version.sh` runs SPRT at `10000ms + 100ms` by default
The script prints that the SPRT block is using `STC` before games start
Rows written by benchmark SPRT runs record `STC` in the dedicated SPRT CSV `time_control` column
`bash -n scripts/benchmark-version.sh` passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Render match time control in version-report
userStories.description: As a developer, I want generated benchmark reports to show match results grouped or labeled by time control so that LTC and STC rows are not merged silently.
acceptanceCriteria: `chess-runner version-report` reads the match CSV `time_control` column
Match-derived report output shows explicit time-control information for each rendered section or grouping
Match aggregation does not silently merge STC and LTC rows for the same opponent
`cargo build --workspace` passes
`cargo test --workspace -- --skip gen_files::magics::name` passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Render SPRT time control in version-report
userStories.description: As a developer, I want generated benchmark reports to show SPRT time control so that STC comparison runs are explicit without opening raw CSV files.
acceptanceCriteria: `chess-runner version-report` reads the SPRT CSV `time_control` column
SPRT-derived report output shows explicit time-control information for rendered rows or sections
SPRT report parsing continues to work with the new CSV schema
`cargo build --workspace` passes
`cargo test --workspace -- --skip gen_files::magics::name` passes
Typecheck passes
